subject: Reading
notes: Core section | 36 questions | 40 minutes | Score range: 1–36 | Shorter passages introduced in 2025

sub_topics: Identifying the central idea or main theme of a passage
Summarising key events, arguments, and information
Understanding explicit and implicit meanings
Drawing logical inferences from the text
Tracking character development in literary narratives
Identifying cause-and-effect relationships
Distinguishing between fact and opinion
Understanding sequential and chronological information
Determining the meaning of words and phrases in context
Analysing the author's purpose and point of view
Evaluating the effectiveness of author's stylistic choices
Understanding text structure (comparison, cause-effect, problem-solution)
Identifying the function of a sentence or paragraph within the whole text
Recognising tone, mood, and rhetorical strategies
Analysing how an argument is developed and supported
Comparing two paired passages on similar themes or topics
Integrating information from text and visual elements (graphs, tables)
Evaluating the quality and relevance of evidence in an argument
Synthesising information across multiple texts
Identifying and evaluating claims and counterclaims

major_topics.weightage: N/A — tested across all three components above
sub_topics: Literary Narrative / Prose Fiction — novels, short stories, personal essays, memoirs
Social Studies — biography, history, anthropology, psychology, sociology, geography, economics
Humanities — art, music, philosophy, architecture, cultural history
Natural Science — meteorology, astronomy, botany, geology, microbiology, anatomy, zoology

subject: Science
notes: OPTIONAL from September 2025 | 40 questions | 40 minutes | Score range: 1–36 | Tests reasoning, not content recall

sub_topics: Reading and interpreting graphs (line graphs, bar charts, scatter plots)
Reading and interpreting data tables
Identifying trends and patterns in data
Interpolating and extrapolating from data sets
Comparing data across experiments or trials
Identifying anomalies or outliers in data
Using data to draw conclusions
Understanding experimental design and methodology
Identifying independent, dependent, and controlled variables
Evaluating the validity and reliability of an experiment
Predicting results of a modified experiment
Identifying sources of error in an experiment
Understanding tools and equipment used in scientific research
Engineering and design topics (new emphasis in 2025 Enhanced ACT)

major_topics.topic_name: Evaluation of Models, Inferences, and Experimental Results
sub_topics: Evaluating and comparing competing scientific hypotheses or models
Identifying assumptions underlying a scientific model
Drawing logical conclusions from experimental results
Recognising strengths and weaknesses of scientific explanations
Resolving conflicting viewpoints using evidence from passages
Predicting what new evidence would support or contradict a hypothesis
Science content areas: biology, chemistry, physics, Earth and space sciences
